commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair branch is needed; maintainers need to coordinate the linked cross-repo slice and own the contact-export privacy boundary before merge.; Needs attention: No supply-chain or code-execution concern was found, but the diff intentionally exposes local contact names and phone numbers through a discoverable metadata command. (sha=23fc1459db256f9a2cdf503351f03c4bfd562599)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `telecrawl [--json] contacts export`, advertises crawlkit `contact-export`, exports stored contacts as `display_name` plus `phone_numbers`, and adds CLI/metadata tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report. The relevant source check is that current main has `telecrawl contacts` but no `contacts export` or metadata `contact-export` command.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair branch is needed; maintainers need to coordinate the linked cross-repo slice and own the contact-export privacy boundary before merge.

target.title: Expose Telegram contact export command
target.body:
## Summary

- add the human CLI `telecrawl [--json] contacts export`
- advertise the machine command `telecrawl --json contacts export` as read-only `contact-export` in crawlkit metadata
- export stored Telegram contacts with only the v0 contract fields: `display_name` and `phone_numbers`
- keep usernames/handles/source ids out of `display_name`, and skip the observed Telegram service contact row shape (`Telegram` / `42777`) so clawdex does not create a fake person for Telegram service notifications

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command. They should land in lockstep; if the command name, metadata argv, envelope, or field names change in one repo, all three should change together.

## Intent

The discoverable local `contact-export` metadata command is intentional. Local opt-in export of stored contact display names and phone numbers is intentional. This is the feature boundary, not an accidental privacy expansion.

The command is local and read-only. It reads the existing local telecrawl archive; it does not fetch remote data and does not include messages, raw paths, source ids, usernames, JIDs/LIDs, interaction counts, ranking signals, or graph/candidate fields.

## Boundary

This is the Telegram producer side of the same contact-import slice as `openclaw/clawdex#2` and `steipete/wacrawl#12`. The crawler owns Telegram-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the metadata-advertised `contact-export` command.

This is intentionally a local CLI/control metadata surface, not a generic crawler-to-crawler protocol, graph layer, or candidate model.

The producer filter is intentionally narrow. It does not claim short phone numbers are invalid. It only skips the observed Telegram service row shape where the stored contact is `full_name=Telegram`, `first_name=Telegram`, `phone=42777`, blank username, blank last name. Tests include a separate short-number person fixture to prove short phone values are not globally rejected.

## Validation

Current head: `23fc1459db256f9a2cdf503351f03c4bfd562599`.

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go nixpkgs#gofumpt -c gofumpt -w internal/cli/cli.go internal/cli/cli_test.go`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go -c go vet ./...`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go -c go test ./...`
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go -c go build ./cmd/telecrawl`
- unit fixture proof: `Short Phone Person` with phone `12345` is exported, while the `Telegram` / `42777` service-shape fixture is not
- copied real Telegram DB edge rows: exactly two `<7` digit source rows, both `Telegram` / `42777`; all exported rows after filtering are ordinary source contacts
- built-binary export smoke against copied real Telegram archive, aggregate only: `{"contacts":198,"telegram_rows":[]}`
- raw copied-DB username proof, aggregate only: `{"with_phone_username":80,"full_name_is_username_casefold":0,"full_name_is_at_username_casefold":0}`
- clawdex import smoke from the built `telecrawl` binary in a copied clawdex HOME, aggregate only: `{"changes":149,"actions":{"create":120,"update":29},"telegram_changes":[],"repeat_changes":0}`
- local ClawSweeper-prompt preflight accepted the username boundary, the narrowed phone/service-row boundary, and clawdex compatibility

## Privacy

The export does not include usernames, JIDs, LIDs, message bodies, raw paths, source row ids, or interaction counts. Proof output reports counts and JSON key shape only; no real contact names or phone numbers are included here.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123
